parameters.content:
## 📄 Automated Invoice Processing (n8n + AI + Google Workspace)

This workflow automatically extracts key invoice data from PDFs uploaded to a Google Drive folder.

It uses **Gmail, Google Drive, Gemini AI, and Google Sheets** to:
- Detect new email from invoicers
- Extract structured information (Supplier, Items, Total, etc.) from the attachment or the body of the e-mail
- Append data into a spreadsheet
- (Send email notifications)

**Here is the mock folder and google sheet to replicate:**
https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1VvF5dGMl1mWXClKMWMJl2q_zlnOW5mQd?usp=share_link

## ⚙️ Setup Before Running
1. Connect your **Google Drive, Google Sheets, Google Gemini (AI) and Gmail** credentials.
2. Update the folder ID in the Google Drive Trigger to match your own 'Invoices' folder.
3. Create or link a Google Sheet to store invoice data.
4. Create or link a Google Sheet to store logs of the automation
5. Modify the emails notification systems

## 📧 Notify When Done

**Sends an email confirmation after successful invoice processing.**

-> Customize the email message in the Gmail node, or disable this step if not needed.

## ✨ Extend This Workflow

### You can expand this automation by:
- Connecting to ERP or CRM systems
- Sending Slack / Telegram alerts
- Adding more error handling and retry logic
- Generate a report for tracking the use of the tool (dashboard, KPI metrics etc.)
- Add another workflow for errors notification (see settings)

parameters.subject: Invoice processed

parameters.content:
## Triggers every minutes and continues when  a new email arrives ⚡️ 
- Take e-mails from all inboxes
- Only unread e-mails 
- **Only e-mails from:**
[SELECT THE SENDER ADRESS]

## Store the attachment on google drive 📑
This step is not mandatory but recommended 
-> select the specific folder for your use

parameters.text:
=## Role and Objective
You are an AI assistant specialized in analyzing emails that contain or attach invoices/bills from the real estate industry (rent invoices, utility bills such as gas, electricity, water, internet, phone, service charges, ground rent, insurance, management fees, etc.). Your task is to read the entire email (subject + body + any text extracted from attached PDFs/images) and extract exactly the same structured information as if the invoice were provided directly.

## Instructions
Extract the following fields when available anywhere in the email (subject line, body, or embedded/attached invoice text). If a field cannot be found, return an empty string ("").

- **AccountNumber**: Tenant/account/customer reference number
- **InvoiceNumber**: Invoice, bill, or statement number
- **ReferenceNumber**: Any alternative reference (property ref, customer ref, etc.)
- **InvoiceDate**: Issue date of the invoice/bill
- **DueDate**: Payment due date or direct debit date
- **BillingPeriodStart**: Start date of the billing/consumption period
- **BillingPeriodEnd**: End date of the billing/consumption period
- **BalanceBroughtForward**: Previous balance carried forward
- **NewCharges**: Total new charges for the current period
- **TotalAmountDue**: Final total amount payable
- **ServiceType**: Type of service being billed (choose among "Rent", "Gas", "Electricity", "Water", "Internet", "Phone", "Service Charge", "Ground Rent", "Insurance", "Management Fee", "Heating", "TV Licence", etc.).  
  → If multiple services, use the primary one or "Mixed/Multiple".
- **SupplierName**: Name of the landlord, property manager, utility provider, or vendor
- **SupplierAddress**: Supplier/landlord/utility company address (if mentioned)
- **PropertyAddress**: The rented property or premises referred to in the email
- **TenantName**: Name of the tenant or company being billed
- **TenantAddress**: Tenant address (if different from property address)
- **PaymentMethod**: Any mentioned payment method (Direct Debit, BACS, bank transfer details, etc.)
- **Items**: All individual charge lines mentioned. For each:
  1. Description (e.g., "Monthly rent", "Gas consumption", "Broadband", "Service charge")
  2. Amount (keep original currency/format)
  3. Period (if specified)
  4. VAT/Tax (if shown separately)
- **TaxAmount**: Total VAT/tax amount (if separately stated)
- **NextMeterReadDate**: Next scheduled meter reading or measurement date
- **Notes**: Any additional important details from the email (payment instructions, contact info, warnings, references, attached file names, etc.)
- **EmailSubject**: Full subject line of the email (for traceability)
- **EmailDate**: Date the email was sent (if visible in the header or body)

## Formatting Rules
- Always return the result in clean JSON format only.
- Use arrays for multiple entries (especially Items).
- If a field is missing → "" (empty string).
- Keep dates and amounts exactly as they appear in the email.
- Do not add commentary or explanations outside the JSON.

## Bad input and errors: 
- If extraction is possible even partially → return clean JSON exactly as before.
- If confidence is too low or document is not a real invoice/bill → return only: `bad input`

### Example Output (JSON)
{
  "AccountNumber": "900412587",
  "InvoiceNumber": "BILL-2025-114523",
  "ReferenceNumber": "PROP-8871",
  "InvoiceDate": "2025-10-05",
  "DueDate": "2025-10-20",
  "BillingPeriodStart": "2025-08-28",
  "BillingPeriodEnd": "2025-09-27",
  "BalanceBroughtForward": "£12.45",
  "NewCharges": "£87.33",
  "TotalAmountDue": "£99.78",
  "ServiceType": "Gas",
  "SupplierName": "British Gas",
  "SupplierAddress": "",
  "PropertyAddress": "Flat 12, Riverside House, 25 Quay Road, Manchester, M1 2AB",
  "TenantName": "TechStart Solutions Ltd",
  "TenantAddress": "",
  "PaymentMethod": "Direct Debit",
  "Items": [
    {
      "Description": "Gas used",
      "Amount": "£67.20",
      "Period": "28/08/2025 - 27/09/2025",
      "VAT": "£3.36"
    },
    {
      "Description": "Standing charge",
      "Amount": "£20.13",
      "Period": "",
      "VAT": "£1.01"
    }
  ],
  "TaxAmount": "£4.37",
  "NextMeterReadDate": "2025-12-27",
  "Notes": "Please find your latest gas bill attached. Estimated reading used this month.",
  "EmailSubject": "Your British Gas bill for Flat 12 Riverside House – £99.78 due 20 Oct",
  "EmailDate": "2025-10-05"
}

options.systemMessage:
=## Role and Objective
You are an assistant specialized in analyzing invoices and bills from the real estate industry (utility bills such as gas/electricity/water, rent invoices, service charge invoices, management fees, etc.). Your role is to extract structured information and organize it according to the specific categories relevant to real estate-related invoicing.

## Instructions
Extract the following fields when available. If a field does not exist or cannot be identified, return an empty string ("").

- **AccountNumber**: Tenant/account/reference number identifying the property or customer
- **InvoiceNumber**: Invoice, bill, or statement number
- **ReferenceNumber**: Any alternative reference (e.g., customer reference, property reference)
- **InvoiceDate**: Date the invoice was issued
- **DueDate**: Payment due date (or direct debit date if applicable)
- **BillingPeriodStart**: Start date of the billing/consumption period
- **BillingPeriodEnd**: End date of the billing/consumption period
- **BalanceBroughtForward**: Previous balance carried forward (if shown)
- **NewCharges**: Total new charges for the current period
- **TotalAmountDue**: Final total amount payable (including any balance brought forward)
- **ServiceType**: Type of service being billed (choose among "Rent", "Gas", "Electricity", "Water", "Internet", "Phone", "Service Charge", "Ground Rent", "Insurance", "Management Fee", "Heating", "TV Licence", etc.).  
  → If multiple services, use the primary one or "Mixed/Multiple".
- **SupplierName**: Name of the landlord, property management company, utility provider, or vendor issuing the invoice
- **SupplierAddress**: Full address of the supplier/landlord/utility company (if shown)
- **PropertyAddress**: The rented property or premises the invoice refers to
- **TenantName**: Name of the tenant or company being billed
- **TenantAddress**: Address of the tenant (if different from property address)
- **PaymentMethod**: Indicated payment method (e.g., Direct Debit, BACS, standing order) and any relevant details
- **Items**: Breakdown of charges. For each charge/line item extract:
  1. Description (choose among "Rent", "Gas consumption", "Service charge", "Ground rent", "Insurance", "Management fee", "Water charges", etc.)
  2. Amount (keep original currency and format)
  3. Period (if specified separately)
  4. VAT/Tax amount (if shown separately)
- **TaxAmount**: Total tax/VAT amount (if separately itemized)
- **NextMeterReadDate**: Next scheduled meter reading or measurement date (common on utility bills)
- **Notes**: Any additional important information, payment instructions, contact details, or references

## Formatting Rules
- Present the extracted information in JSON format.
- Use arrays for multiple entries (especially for Items).
- If a category is missing on the invoice, return it as an empty string ("").
- Keep text concise.
- Dates should be kept in the same format as found on the invoice (e.g., “2025-09-29” or “29 Sep 2025”).
- Amounts should retain the same currency and format as on the invoice (e.g., "£1,250.00" or "€1.250,00").

## Bad input and errors: 
- If extraction is possible even partially → return clean JSON exactly as before.
- Only if confidence is too low or email is not a real invoice/billing email → return `bad input' in every field of the json

### Example Output (JSON)
{
  "AccountNumber": "900412587",
  "InvoiceNumber": "BILL-2025-114523",
  "ReferenceNumber": "PROP-8871",
  "InvoiceDate": "2025-10-05",
  "DueDate": "2025-10-20",
  "BillingPeriodStart": "2025-08-28",
  "BillingPeriodEnd": "2025-09-27",
  "BalanceBroughtForward": "£12.45",
  "NewCharges": "£87.33",
  "TotalAmountDue": "£99.78",
  "ServiceType": "Gas",
  "SupplierName": "British Gas",
  "SupplierAddress": "",
  "PropertyAddress": "Flat 12, Riverside House, 25 Quay Road, Manchester, M1 2AB",
  "TenantName": "TechStart Solutions Ltd",
  "TenantAddress": "",
  "PaymentMethod": "Direct Debit",
  "Items": [
    {
      "Description": "Gas used",
      "Amount": "£67.20",
      "Period": "28/08/2025 - 27/09/2025",
      "VAT": "£3.36"
    },
    {
      "Description": "Standing charge",
      "Amount": "£20.13",
      "Period": "",
      "VAT": "£1.01"
    }
  ],
  "TaxAmount": "£4.37",
  "NextMeterReadDate": "2025-12-27",
  "Notes": "Please find your latest gas bill attached. Estimated reading used this month.",
  "EmailSubject": "Your British Gas bill for Flat 12 Riverside House – £99.78 due 20 Oct",
  "EmailDate": "2025-10-05"
}

nodes.name: Log the processing of the doc

parameters.content: ## Gather information from e-mail and passes the invoice 📲

## Information extraction from doc ℹ️
- Gemini 2.5 flash reads the document and extracts every item

## Switch path  ↔
1. UPPER PATH = The email has an attachment
2. LOWER PATH = The email doesn't have any attachment

## Logs 👨🏼‍💻
1. Logging of the processing of the e-mail into another table to track the activity of the automation
2. Set the e-mail as "Read"
## Gather information from e-mail and passes the invoice 📲

parameters.subject: Error in invoice processing
Error in invoice processing

parameters.content:
## Error detector 🪳
If there was an error, a notification is sent by email 
-> you can custom the notification system for an error (recipient, body etc)

## Input formatting 🔁

After the AI returns structured data, the fields are separated

- `Code in JavaScript`: Formats the data for insertion into Google Sheets.

### Then insertion of data into google sheet table
